# Code of Conduct

## Our pledge

Paneflow should be a useful, welcoming project for people building and using
agent-first developer tools. We expect contributors and maintainers to keep
project spaces professional, inclusive, and focused on the work.

## Expected behavior

- Be respectful and assume good intent.
- Critique ideas, code, and tradeoffs rather than people.
- Keep issues and pull requests on topic.
- Give enough context for others to reproduce, review, or discuss your point.
- Respect maintainers' time: prefer small, actionable reports and PRs.

## Unacceptable behavior

- Harassment, insults, personal attacks, or discriminatory language.
- Trolling, repeated off-topic comments, or bad-faith argument loops.
- Publishing another person's private information.
- Any behavior that would make contributors feel unsafe participating.

## Enforcement

Maintainers may edit, hide, or remove comments; close threads; block users; or
limit participation when behavior harms the project. Enforcement decisions are
made to protect contributors and keep the project productive.

## Reporting

If you see behavior that violates this code, email **arthur.jean@strivex.fr**
with a short description and links or screenshots when available.
